data.title: Bybit’s stolen assets account for about 15% of the total amount stolen in the cryptocurrency industry
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News According to the statistics of the hacker section of the DefiLlama website, Bybit’s stolen assets of more than 1.4 billion US dollars are the largest security incident in the history of cryptocurrency, accounting for about 15% of the stolen amount in all security incidents in the history of cryptocurrency. The panel data shows that the cumulative amount of stolen assets in the history of cryptocurrency exceeds 10.62 billion US dollars, of which DeFi-related stolen assets amount to 6.31 billion US dollars, and various cross-bridge projects have stolen assets of up to 2.87 billion US dollars.

data.title: BAN briefly broke through 0.17 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of 65.71%
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Bitget market data shows that BAN briefly broke through 0.17 USDT and is currently trading at 0.1508 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of 65.71%.

data.title: Tim Wong, Chairman of Catizen Foundation: I believe Bybit will successfully overcome the challenges
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Catizen Foundation Chairman Tim Wong said on Twitter that he firmly believes that Bybit will successfully overcome the current challenges. As one of Bybit's important partners, Tim reviewed Bybit's valuable support in the early stages of Catizen's development, especially in terms of marketing and resource integration.
Tim Wong emphasized that although Bybit is currently facing some challenges, he believes that Bybit will quickly resolve the current difficulties and draw strength from them to continue to grow and develop. The tweet reads: "What doesn't kill you will only make you stronger."

data.title: ZachXBT: Bybit hacking incident is related to Phemex hacking incident, and the related addresses overlap
Stablecoin Yield Farming: A Safe Bet for Passive Income?
data.description:
TL;DR Yield farming with stablecoins allows investors to earn passive income by leveraging the stability of fiat-backed currencies, offering higher rates than traditional methods. It involves depositing these coins into DeFi platforms to generate returns, but it carries risks such as smart contract vulnerabilities and impermanent loss. The growth of the DeFi ecosystem and options ... 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/stablecoin-yield-farming-a-safe-bet-for-passive-income/

data.title: Bybit CEO Reports Fund Transfers to Chainflip.io for Bitcoin Conversion
data.description:
According to PANews, Bybit CEO Ben Zhou has reported that certain funds have been transferred to Chainflip.io for conversion into Bitcoin. The transactions involved are as follows:

- 0x4f5f7ba657bf518d383828183087978b452b99da6cde0c9b94739b8d72a8c5ef

- 0x1e71b458812c91ce7c49922d9e966ba99cda1a1f017c8dfabb31f560a67ddfcc

- 0x3ff650d457ce3edba4a05b07d60360bb571f496b0ff506abf77cacbbce04e6b2

- 0xdc505d2661f8bc9429a4bed354c2ccfefb15013477efd7e6f578c0e37340446a

Zhou has urged cross-chain bridge projects to assist in blocking and preventing further conversions to other chains. Bybit plans to announce a bounty program soon for anyone who helps in blocking or tracing the funds, leading to their recovery.

data.title: Bybit Hacker Utilizes Chainflip for Cross-Chain Asset Conversion
data.description: According to PANews, on-chain analyst Yu Jin has reported that the Bybit hacker has begun using the Chainflip cross-chain exchange tool to convert ETH into assets on other blockchains. Approximately 40 minutes ago, the hacker dispersed 5,000 ETH across multiple addresses and has since laundered 205 ETH through Chainflip.

data.title: Lazarus Group Transfers 5000 ETH Amid Bybit Incident
data.description: According to Odaily, ZachXBT reported on his personal channel that the Lazarus Group, involved in the Bybit incident, has transferred 5000 ETH to a new address. The group allegedly laundered the funds through eXch, a centralized mixer, and bridged the assets to Bitcoin using Chainflip. The transaction occurred on February 22, 2025, at 6:28:23 AM UTC+8, with the transaction ID 0xbf80907830e46317da2c1708a13a9f016e242f8a6db6e6b0706ea5f2328cb001. The final Bitcoin address through Chainflip is bc1qlu4a33zjspefa3tnq566xszcr0fvwz05ewhqfq.

data.title: OKX President: Bybit hackers have been blacklisted and are willing to provide security and liquidity support for Bybit
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: OKX President Hong Fang posted on the X platform that "The hacker addresses of the Bybit theft case have been added to OKX's blacklist. The team engineers are closely monitoring these addresses and will take immediate action if there are any changes in funds. Our team is also in contact with the bybit team to provide any IT security support and liquidity support we can provide at this stage."

data.title: US Senators question SEC’s stance on staking in ETPs while Hester Pierce promises more clarity
data.description:
Hester Pierce, the SEC commissioner, promises more clarity and says she's open to permitting staking features for ETH-USD ETFs.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/us-senators-demand-sec-clarification-etp/
